scenario: all right , how have you been ?
patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "hey , how are you today ?"] all right , how have you been ?
[Clinician asked: "i'm good ."] good , good .
[Clinician asked: "have you lost some weight or are you at least putting on some muscle ? you look trim ."] no , i think i'm pretty much the same as i've always been .
[Clinician asked: "really ? okay , maybe it's just your black shirt . makes you look thin ."] yeah , i guess that's it .
[Clinician asked: "so health wise , how is everything going ?"] good , the testosterone's going well .

[Clinician asked: "that's great ."] uh , it helped me out . i feel good , more vigorous , sleeping well and i think it's having some positive effects . not so much physically because like i said i've- i've been this way my whole life , but i'm seeing some good improvements in my bloodwork .
[Clinician asked: "okay , well that's good ."] so the finasteride i'm only taking half a pill , it's the 5 milligram one .

[Clinician asked: "yeah , i remember you telling me that ."] and cialis , on the days i work out i take 5 milligrams otherwise i take two and a half milligram pills , but , uh , i have been out of it .
[Clinician asked: "okay ."] but overall i'm doing well , i'm actually taking the correct steps to get my life together .
[Clinician asked: "good . it's always great to hear . well let's take a look . uhm , i'm gon na listen to your heart and lungs ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "please use my general exam template , all right . just take a few breaths ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "in and out ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "all right , everything sounds good , no concerns there ."] great . so i wanted to show you something .
[Clinician asked: "sure ."] look at this .

[Clinician asked: "okay , this is your cholesterol ?"] yeah , my cholesterol and triglycerides . uh , i used to have high triglycerides , you see they were 265 milligrams per deciliter , and i took my first dose of the testosterone on the 28th .
[Clinician asked: "right ."] now 5 months later look at my numbers .
[Clinician asked: "wow , that's remarkable ."] is it the test ? it's the only change .

[Clinician asked: "i do n't know , i have n't honestly seen many guys over the years that have cholesterol problems and this . i mean there's a big correlation between diabetic control and testosterone replacement , meaning those who get good levels of their test see their diabetic control improve ."] yeah .

[Clinician asked: "but i have n't seen a lot of data on the impact on cholesterol . regardless , we will take it ."] i agree . i was very impressed with my triglycerides and was just wondering if the test may be what's helping .
[Clinician asked: "yeah , that's an unbelievable difference ."] 145 milligrams per deciliter from 265 milligrams per deciliter is awesome . i also read that it- it's cardioprotective .
[Clinician asked: "absolutely ."] my red blood cell count has increased .
[Clinician asked: "yeah , i saw that . it's fine though ."] stable .
[Clinician asked: "your psa today is also , uh , is good also . it's , uh , .6 i think ."] yeah . , is it ?
[Clinician asked: "yeah , it was .5 last year and anything under 4 is good ."] nice , that's good news .

[Clinician asked: "so it just needs to be checked every year or so ."] so in terms of , uh , estrogen control i've been hearing that indole-3-carbinol , or broccoli extract , supposedly can improve my estrogen levels . have you ever heard of it ?
[Clinician asked: "yeah , i've heard of it but i have n't had anybody consistently use it . i mean , your levels are fine and we checked your estra- estradiol and it was not elevated , so ."] okay .

[Clinician asked: "i would argue that we could test that in the fall if you want , but we do n't need to do , uh , do any more tests ... any more than test once a year , excuse me ."] okay , what about increasing my testosterone to 175 milligrams ? i'm at 140 now .
[Clinician asked: "well , your levels are high ."] are they right now ?
[Clinician asked: "well , i mean they were last time ."] yeah but i just- just injected though , or i had right before that was taken .
[Clinician asked: "i know . i know you had then , uh , when did you inject this time ?"] i figure i'm on my eighth day today .
[Clinician asked: "okay ."] so i'm due to dose on thursday or friday .
[Clinician asked: "all right ."] i have a little med calendar and i put checks and ts on it . that helps me .

[Clinician asked: "that's a great idea . so look , the biggest issue i've seen , even if your levels today are around 700 , is that your peaks are getting greater than 1,500 , putting you at a higher chance of needing to come off due to blood thickness . and your risk will only astronomically go up the higher the dose"] okay .
[Clinician asked: "you look well , your levels are good and you're feeling well ."] yeah , i'm feeling good .

[Clinician asked: "i'm going to be blunt . unfortunately this happens often where you're feeling good but you want to feel really good . i mean , i get it and this is why people get into problems with this stuff , right ? it's like , back in the day when it was n't prescribed by doctors and people would get it at gyms"] yeah , they have to be taking a lot .
[Clinician asked: "likely they are taking more than testosterone , but still ."] and they are taking stuff for a long time .
[Clinician asked: "true . but right now i would not change your dose ."] okay .
[Clinician asked: "make sense ?"] it does , i appreciate the discussion .
[Clinician asked: "no problem . what pharmacy are you using ? have you changed it or anything ?"] no changes , i use walmart pharmacy . i do need more cialis and finasteride .
[Clinician asked: "okay ."] i would prefer the paper prescription .
[Clinician asked: "for all of them ?"] sure .
[Clinician asked: "all right , will do . i'm gon na get your prescriptions ."] okay , thank you .
gold_note:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Hypogonadism.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Mr. Paul Edwards is a 59-year-old male, an established patient, who presents to the clinic today for hypogonadism. He was last seen on 11/24/2020. The patient’s history includes positive for HIV, and today he is concerned with gynecomastia.

The patient is doing well, overall, and feels the testosterone is helping. He reports weight stability, feels lively, good, more vigorous, and he is sleeping well. He also thinks the testosterone is having a positive effect on his blood work. He endorses that his triglycerides have always been elevated and adds that they were as high as 265 mg/dL. The patient presented a copy of his bloodwork, showing a significant decrease in his cholesterol after 5 months. Mr. Edwards questioned if the changes in his blood work were due to the testosterone. He also noted that his red blood cell count has not increased. The patient inquired about possible benefits of Indole-3-carbinol, which is a broccoli extract for estrogen control.

Currently, the patient is dosing 140 ng/dL of testosterone per week and inquired if his dose could be increased to 175 ng/dL. He recalled that he had recently injected testosterone before his last testosterone blood work was performed; last testosterone levels were greater than 1500 ng/dL. The patient confirms continued daily use of Finasteride and Cialis, prescribed by me, and stated he needs a refill for both.

Mr. Edwards mentioned he is taking correct steps to get his life "together." He also uses a med calendar to help with medication compliance.

PAST HISTORY

Medical
HIV

SOCIAL HISTORY

Utilizes med calendar to support medication compliance.
Actively trying to get his life together.

CURRENT MEDICATIONS

Cialis 5 mg tablet by mouth on days he exercises, 2.5 mg tablet by mouth on days without exercise.
Finasteride 5 mg half tablet daily.
Testosterone cypionate 140 mg.

RESULTS

PSA 0.6 ng/mL.
Triglycerides 145 mg/dL.
Total testosterone 1500 ng/dL, 11/24/2021.

ASSESSMENT

• Hypogonadism.

PLAN

I recommend maintaining current management. The patient’s last testosterone levels were greater than 1500 ng/dL, although this level was likely related to his recent injection. He requested an increase to his testosterone cypionate from 140 mg to 175 mg, which I denied due to his already high testosterone levels. I counseled the patient on the risks associated with high testosterone levels and Indole-3-carbinol and it’s lacking evidence to support his estrogen levels. The last time his estradiol levels were checked, they were normal; recommend annual screening.

I provided paper prescription refills for Cialis and Finasteride.

naive.response_text:
It's great to hear about the positive changes you've noticed in your cholesterol and triglycerides. While testosterone might be contributing to these improvements, it's also possible that other factors like your diet and exercise routine are playing a role. Keep up the good work, and let's continue to monitor these levels closely.

Regarding your request to increase the testosterone dose, I understand your desire to feel even better. However, it's important to be cautious, as higher doses can increase the risk of blood thickness and other side effects. For now, let's maintain your current dose and continue to monitor your levels and overall health. If you're interested in adjusting your dose in the future, we can revisit this discussion after reviewing your lab results again.

Thank you for being so proactive about your health. Keep up the excellent work, and don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions or concerns. I'll provide the necessary prescriptions and we'll continue to keep a close eye on everything.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thank you for sharing your health journey with me. It's clear you're proud of the improvements you've seen, especially with your cholesterol levels dropping from 265 to 145 mg/dL. That's a remarkable change, and it's understandable you're curious about the role testosterone might be playing. I can see how excited you are about these positive results and how motivated you are to continue taking care of yourself.

We discussed the possibility of increasing your testosterone dose to 175 mg, but it's important to consider the risks as the dose goes up. Higher doses can increase the chance of blood thickness and other side effects. I understand you want to feel even better, but let's be cautious and monitor your levels closely. We can always revisit this in a few months if you're stable and feeling well.

Regarding indole-3-carbinol, while it's an interesting supplement, there's limited evidence on its effectiveness for estrogen balance in men. Since your estrogen levels are currently within normal range, we might not need to address this unless you start experiencing symptoms. However, if you're interested in exploring this further, we can consider testing in the fall.

Let's plan to check in in 4-6 weeks to review your labs and how you're feeling. Before you leave, could you summarize what we've agreed on today? That way, I can ensure everything is clear and we're on the same page.

Thank you for being such a proactive patient. I'm here to support you as you continue on this journey. Take care, and I'll see you in a few weeks.
